rulings.Case Title: VI LE VS. LUCAS FAMILY TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 22, 2025, line 1. Defendants' Motion for Attorney's Fees is OFF CALENDAR for failure to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of the moving papers. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PALACE COURT APARTMENTS, LLC VS. SEAN SHARP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement And Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 22, 2025, line 5. Plaintiff's Motion for Enforcement of Settlement Agreement and Entry of Judgment is OFF CALENDAR. Defendant is represented limited scope; no substitution of attorney or order relieving the limited scope attorney is on file. As such, all papers in the case must be served both on the defendant's limited scope attorney and on the defendant. (CRC 3.36.) The proofs of service indicate that the moving papers were served on the defendant only. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: OM/UDR SF LLC, A DELAWARE VS. JUANIECE RAINEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 22, 2025, line 6. Plaintiff's Motion to Set Aside Dismissal is DENIED. Plaintiff has not shown that the alleged mistake in filing a request for dismissal of this action was reasonable so as to warrant discretionary relief under CCP 473(b). rulings.Case Title: WESTSIDE COURTS HOUSING PARTNERS, L.P. VS. KAMARI DAWSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Of Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment; Proposed Response; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for July 22, 2025, line 7. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Default and Judgment is GRANTED. Plaintiff concedes the default should be set aside. As such, the default judgment and the writ issued thereon must also be vacated and recalled. The default of defendant Kamari Dawson is set aside. The default judgment entered on April 3, 2025 is vacated. The writ issued on May 15, 2025 is recalled. Defendant Kamari Dawson shall file and serve the proposed response within five days. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
